claimText: The Himalayan Outpost for Planetary Exploration (HOPE) is an analog mission inaugurated by ISRO in the Tso Kar Valley, Ladakh.
evidenceExcerpt: ISRO inaugurated Himalayan Outpost for Planetary Exploration (HOPE) Analog Mission in Tso Kar Valley, Ladakh.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the space agency and geographical location associated with the HOPE analog mission.
distractorHooks: Attributing the mission to NASA, ESA, or Roscosmos instead of ISRO
Placing the mission in Spiti Valley, Thar Desert, or Antarctica instead of Tso Kar Valley, Ladakh
Confusing it with the UAE's Hope Mars Mission

claimText: The HOPE Analog Mission infrastructure includes interconnected Habitat and Utility modules designed for crew living, operations, and support systems.
evidenceExcerpt: A specially designed Habitat Module for crew living and a Utility Module for operations and support systems which are interconnected for seamless workflow.
examUse: Statement-based question on the structural components and crew capabilities of the HOPE analog mission.
distractorHooks: Claiming the mission is purely robotic with no crew habitat modules
Stating the modules are completely isolated from each other rather than interconnected
